content.full_text:
89213

4-76-342

August 20, 1976

Ms. Dianna Lyons
Post Office Box 62
Keene, California 93531

Dear Ms. Lyons:

     This letter is to confirm my advice to you in our telephone
conversation August 17, 1976.

     You asked whether the State Citizens Participation Committee
may form a subcommittee whose name, Yes on 14 would more easily be
identified by voters with a position on Proposition 14 on the
November 1976 ballot. You stated that separate financial records
would be maintained by the subcommittee.

     I advised that the State Citizens Participation Committee may
form a subcommittee, and that the subcommittee should register with
the Secretary of State's office. In Part III of Form 410, the trea-
surer should indicate that Yes on 14 will act jointly with the pri-
mary committee. I also stated that, pursuant to Government Code
Section 84213, the two committees may file consolidated campaign
statements.

     If you have further questions, please contact our staff.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Nan Hambleton
                                        Special Compliance Representative

NH:bw

bcc: Jeanne P.


Due to the quality of the image and the reversed/faded nature of the text, I cannot provide an accurate verbatim transcription while following the instruction to copy the text exactly as presented. The document appears to contain multiple paragraphs, but the specific words are not legible enough to transcribe with confidence.
